title: Hemophilia A
chief_complaint: My knee is swollen, hot, and incredibly painful. I bumped it slightly playing basketball. I can't move it at all. I've had 'bleeding problems' since childhood - I bruise easily and have had joint bleeds before.

physical_exam.Right Knee: Severely swollen, warm, tender. Held in flexion. Range of motion severely limited by pain. Ballotable patella (large effusion).
physical_exam.Skin: Multiple ecchymoses at various stages on arms and legs. No petechiae (differentiates from platelet disorders).
physical_exam.Musculoskeletal: Left knee: Flexion contracture from previous hemarthroses. Right ankle: Chronic arthropathy with limited range of motion (target joint).

hidden_data.pt_ptt: PT 12 (normal), aPTT 68 (prolonged - normal 25-35), PTT mixing study: CORRECTS (rules out inhibitor).
hidden_data.factor_assays: Factor VIII activity <1% (severe hemophilia A). Factor IX normal. Von Willebrand factor normal (rules out VWD).
hidden_data.inhibitor: Bethesda assay: No Factor VIII inhibitor present.
hidden_data.cbc: WBC 8,500, Hb 11.5 (mild anemia from recent bleeding), Platelets 260,000
hidden_data.joint_xray: Right knee: Large effusion. No fracture. Left knee: Joint space narrowing, osteophytes, subchondral cysts (chronic hemophilic arthropathy).
hidden_data.genetic: F8 gene intron 22 inversion (most common mutation in severe hemophilia A)

correct_diagnosis: Severe Hemophilia A (Factor VIII <1%) with Acute Hemarthrosis (Right Knee) and Chronic Hemophilic Arthropathy
key_learning_points: Hemophilia A = X-linked recessive deficiency of Factor VIII. Severity: Mild 5-40%, Moderate 1-5%, Severe <1% factor activity
Clinical: HEMARTHROSIS is hallmark (knee > elbow > ankle). Muscle hematomas (psoas, calf - can cause compartment syndrome). Easy bruising. NO petechiae (differentiates from platelet disorders)
Lab: Prolonged aPTT, normal PT, normal platelets. Factor VIII assay confirms. If mixing study doesn't correct = Factor VIII inhibitor (alloantibody) - most serious complication
Treatment: Factor VIII concentrate (recombinant or plasma-derived). Dosing: 1 unit/kg raises FVIII 2%. Target: Hemarthrosis 40-50% for 1-3 days. Major bleed/surgery 80-100% for 7-14 days
Prophylaxis: Regular factor infusions 2-3x/week prevent joint damage. Desmopressin (DDAVP) only for mild hemophilia A (releases stored FVIII). Aminocaproic acid for mucosal bleeding
